title: Food Preparation Workers
description: Perform a variety of food preparation duties other than cooking, such as preparing cold foods and shellfish, slicing meat, and brewing coffee or tea.

tasks.text: Clean and sanitize work areas, equipment, utensils, dishes, or silverware.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist cooks and kitchen staff with various tasks as needed, and provide cooks with needed items.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take and record temperature of food and food storage areas, such as refrigerators and freezers.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Carry food supplies, equipment, and utensils to and from storage and work areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove trash and clean kitchen garbage containers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Store food in designated containers and storage areas to prevent spoilage.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Weigh or measure ingredients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Vacuum dining area and sweep and mop kitchen floor.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inform supervisors when equipment is not working properly and when food and supplies are getting low, and order needed items.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Wash, peel, and cut various foods, such as fruits and vegetables, to prepare for cooking or serving.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare a variety of foods, such as meats, vegetables, or desserts, according to customers' orders or supervisors' instructions, following approved procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble meal trays with foods in accordance with patients' diets.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Stock cupboards and refrigerators, and tend salad bars and buffet meals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Use manual or electric appliances to clean, peel, slice, and trim foods.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load dishes, glasses, and tableware into dishwashing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Portion and wrap food, or place it directly on plates for service to patrons.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Add cutlery, napkins, food, and other items to trays on assembly lines in hospitals, cafeterias, airline kitchens, and similar establishments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place food trays over food warmers for immediate service, or store them in refrigerated storage cabinets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare and serve a variety of beverages, such as coffee, tea, and soft drinks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix ingredients for green salads, molded fruit salads, vegetable salads, and pasta salads.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Receive and store food supplies, equipment, and utensils in refrigerators, cupboards, and other storage areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Stir and strain soups and sauces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Make special dressings and sauces as condiments for sandwiches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Scrape leftovers from dishes into garbage containers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Distribute food to waiters and waitresses to serve to customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate cash register, handle money, and give correct change.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Distribute menus to hospital patients, collect diet sheets, and deliver food trays and snacks to nursing units or directly to patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Package take-out foods or serve food to customers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut, slice or grind meat, poultry, and seafood to prepare for cooking.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Butcher and clean fowl, fish, poultry, and shellfish to prepare for cooking or serving.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep records of the quantities of food used.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.
